meta.title: Bahasa Indonesia Affixes — Batch 2
meta.description: A second multiple-choice deck covering combined and less common Indonesian affixes (ber-...-an, memper-...-kan/-i, di-...-i/-kan, ordinal ke-/per-, -nya, -wan/-wati) with real example words.

cards.question: Circumfix 'ber-...-an' in 'bersalaman' (from 'salam', meaning 'greeting') expresses...
choices: a reciprocal action - people shaking hands / greeting each other
a passive action of being greeted
cards.explanation: Ber-...-an often marks reciprocal actions performed mutually by two or more parties: 'bersalaman' means to shake hands with one another.

cards.question: Circumfix 'memper-...-kan' in 'mempersiapkan' (from 'siap', meaning 'ready') indicates...
choices: a passive verb meaning 'to be readied by someone'
a causative verb meaning 'to prepare/get something ready (often thoroughly, for a purpose)'
cards.explanation: Memper-...-kan combines the causative memper- with -kan to intensify the causative sense: 'mempersiapkan' means to actively prepare something.

cards.question: Circumfix 'di-...-i' in 'dikunjungi' (from 'kunjung', meaning 'visit') indicates...
choices: passive voice with a locative sense - 'to be visited (by someone)'
cards.explanation: Di- marks the passive voice, and combined with -i it retains the locative sense of the base verb: 'dikunjungi' means 'to be visited'.

cards.question: Circumfix 'di-...-kan' in 'disiapkan' (from 'siap', meaning 'ready') indicates...
cards.explanation: Di- forms the passive counterpart of causative meN-...-kan verbs, so 'disiapkan' means 'to be prepared (by someone)'.

cards.question: Prefix 'ke-' in 'ketiga' (from 'tiga', meaning 'three') here functions to...
cards.explanation: Ke- attached to a cardinal number produces its ordinal form: 'tiga' (three) becomes 'ketiga' (third).

cards.question: The word 'pertama' (meaning 'first') uses 'per-' in the same ordinal-forming role as which pattern?
cards.explanation: 'Pertama' is an irregular ordinal ('first') built with per- rather than ke-, but it serves the same ordinal-number function as ke- does in words like 'ketiga'.

cards.question: Suffix '-nya' in 'rumahnya' (from 'rumah', meaning 'house') most likely means...
choices: his/her house, or 'the house' (marking possession or definiteness)
cards.explanation: -nya is a versatile suffix marking third-person possession or definiteness: 'rumahnya' means 'his/her house' or, in context, 'the house'.

cards.question: Combination 'se-...-nya' in 'sebisanya' (from 'bisa', meaning 'can/able') expresses...
choices: 'to the best of one's ability / as much as one can'
cards.explanation: Se-...-nya on an ability word forms an idiomatic 'as ... as possible' construction: 'sebisanya' means 'to the best of one's ability'.

cards.question: Circumfix 'ke-...-an' in 'kehujanan' (from 'hujan', meaning 'rain') here conveys...
choices: an abstract noun meaning 'the concept of rain'
being accidentally caught in the rain (an unintended, unfortunate experience)
a command to make it rain
cards.explanation: With certain weather/misfortune words, ke-...-an takes on an accidental/adversative sense rather than forming an abstract noun: 'kehujanan' means to get caught in the rain.

cards.question: Circumfix 'memper-...-i' in 'mempelajari' (from 'ajar', meaning 'teach/learn') indicates...
choices: a passive verb meaning 'to be taught'
a causative/thorough verb meaning 'to study/learn something in depth'
a reciprocal action between teacher and student
cards.explanation: Memper-...-i intensifies the base and directs it thoroughly at an object: 'mempelajari' means 'to study something in depth'.

cards.question: Prefix 'pe-' in 'pemarah' (from 'marah', meaning 'angry') describes...
choices: a one-time act of getting angry
a person with a habitually short temper - 'a hot-tempered person'
a passive verb meaning 'to be angered'
cards.explanation: Pe- attached to certain adjectives forms nouns describing a person with that trait as a habitual characteristic: 'pemarah' means a hot-tempered person.

cards.question: Suffix '-wan' in 'wartawan' (from 'warta', meaning 'news/information') forms a noun meaning...
cards.explanation: -wan (borrowed from Sanskrit) forms nouns denoting a person associated with or skilled in the root concept: 'wartawan' means 'journalist'.

cards.question: Suffix '-wati' in 'peragawati' (from 'peraga', meaning 'demonstrator/display') forms a noun meaning...
cards.explanation: -wati is the feminine counterpart of -wan, forming nouns for women associated with the root concept: 'peragawati' means 'female model'.

cards.question: Circumfix 'ber-...-an' in 'berjatuhan' (from 'jatuh', meaning 'fall') expresses...
choices: multiple things falling one after another / scattered falling
a passive verb meaning 'to be dropped'
cards.explanation: Beyond reciprocal meaning, ber-...-an can also mark a plural or scattered occurrence of an action: 'berjatuhan' describes many things falling one after another.

cards.question: Combination 'ter-...-kan' in 'terselesaikan' (from 'selesai', meaning 'finished') expresses...
choices: something that has been resolved/finished, often without deliberate effort or as a final outcome
cards.explanation: Ter- combined with a -kan causative base gives a resultative/potential passive sense: 'terselesaikan' means 'resolved/gets finished', often implying it happened eventually or without full deliberate control.
